response.justification: Candidate has exceptional domain expertise in geospatial and navigation systems (GISCollective, Green Map), with solid distributed systems experience from infrastructure roles. However, the primary technical gap is significant: C++ commercial experience ends in 2015 (over 10 years ago), followed by a decade primarily in JavaScript/TypeScript. While the learning agility and domain knowledge are strong assets, the rustiness in the core technical requirement and lack of modern systems programming work create meaningful risk that would require careful assessment during interview.
strengths: Deep domain expertise in geospatial data, GIS platforms, and navigation systems (GISCollective founder, FOSS4G speaker, Green Map contributor)
Proven distributed systems experience handling millions of requests, infrastructure setup, and understanding of fault tolerance and service architecture
Demonstrated full-stack learning agility across web, mobile, desktop, embedded systems, and infrastructure—shows ability to master new domains quickly
concerns: C++ experience is 10+ years stale (last role: NI LabWindows 2014-2015); recent decade focused on JavaScript/TypeScript and web development
Recent work is in high-level web/product engineering (TypeScript, Node.js, React) rather than systems-level or performance-critical C++ applications
No Rust experience listed, despite Rust being an acceptable alternative to C++ in the job requirements
